message:
fix(fetch): handle HTTP status=0 correctly in Response class

The Response constructor used `init.status || 200` which treated status=0
(network error) as falsy and defaulted to 200. This caused SDK code to
attempt JSON.parse on empty responses, resulting in "Unexpected end of
JSON input" errors.

Changes:
- Use explicit `init.status !== undefined` check instead of ||
- Add empty body handling in json() for status=0 case
- Clean up debug output from polyfills (gate behind _edgeboxDebug flag)
- Add util/types module for Node.js compatibility
- Clean up H2 client debug output in vendor/metal0
